chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.6080 of 2015 Before: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah Shoaib Rasool and another.................................... Petitioners Versus Federation of Pakistan & others............................ Respondent Date of Hearing: 04.03.2020 Date of Decision: 04.03.2020 Mr. Bashir Ahmed Channa, advocate for the petitioners. ------ O R D E R IRSHAD ALI SHAH, J:- The petitioners are alleged to have purchased the property as is detailed in the petition out of crime proceeds, therefore, they were served with the notice u/s 9 of AMLA, Act 2010 by Mr.Siraj Panhwar, Inspector FIA Corporate Crime Circle Karachi, to show cause within thirty days of receipt of such notices as to why such property should not be declared to have been purchased out of crime proceeds and money laundering. Such show cause notices have been challenged by the petitioners to be declared illegal and unlawful by this Court by way of instant petition. The issuance of show cause notice, if any, is a legal proceedings, same could not be declared to be illegal and unlawful by this Court in summary manner. If the petitioners are carrying a feeling that they are innocent then they could contest such notices by furnishing their replies before investigating officer. If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that 2 the petitioners are apprehending their unjustified arrest at the hands of police, then they could seek pre-arrest bail from the Court having jurisdiction in accordance with law by joining the investigation. No case for indulgence in subject show cause notices by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction is made out.

If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that 2 the petitioners are apprehending their unjustified arrest at the hands of police, then they could seek pre-arrest bail from the Court having jurisdiction in accordance with law by joining the investigation. No case for indulgence in subject show cause notices by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction is made out. Consequently the instant petition is dismissed together with the listed applications. JUDGE JUDGE
